title: spatialdataR
text: spatialdataR package provides R interface Python’s spatialdata framework unified handling spatial omics data, including tabular annotations, vector- raster-based components. Developed part scverse project (Virshup et al. 2023), spatialdata aims solve challenges integrating diverse spatial datasets – including – employing OME-NGFF (Next Generation File Format) standard (Marconato et al. 2025). Python implementation core specifications found official spatialdata website.

title: spatialdataR
text: core data structure SpatialData class, organizes data 5 coordinated layers: images, labels, points, shapes, tables. layer stored list layer-specific objects carry associated SpatialDataAttr (@meta slot), encode spatialdata-specific Zarr attributes (.zattr Zarr v2, zarr.json Zarr v3). Together, layers provide unified representation spatial omics data, combining raster, vector, tabular data within single coherent framework. Images/labels store raster-based data multi-scale, multi-channel arrays (e.g., immunofluorescent images segmentation masks). represented SpatialDataImage/Label objects , turn, inherit SpatialDataArray. backed list ZarrArrays via Rarr ZarrArray, enabling chunked, -disk access. Points/shapes represent spatial coordinates geometric regions (e.g., transcript locations segmentation boundaries). represented SpatialDataPoint/Shape objects , turn, inherit SpatialDataFrame. DuckDB-backed duckspatial_df, enabling efficient lazy handling. Tables store functional annotations information aggregated across layers (e.g., gene ×\times cell data). currently represented -memory SingleCellExperiment objects; delayed, Zarr-backed handling assay data active development.

title: spatialdataR
text: SpatialData represented -disk Zarr stores. package provides readSpatialData() function ingest entire store, although arguments control layers elements read read also available. demonstration, use toy dataset included package: output summarizes SpatialData object, showing dimension elements layer (images, labels, points, shapes, tables), well defined coordinate systems elements align .
code: # dependencies library(spatialdataR) library(SingleCellExperiment)  # path to 'spatialdata' Zarr store zs <- file.path("extdata", "blobs.zarr") zs <- system.file(zs, package="spatialdataR")  # read as 'SpatialData' R object (sd <- readSpatialData(zs)) ## class: SpatialData ## - images(2): ##   - blobs_image (3,64,64) ##   - blobs_multiscale_image (3,64,64) ## - labels(2): ##   - blobs_labels (64,64) ##   - blobs_multiscale_labels (64,64) ## - points(1): ##   - blobs_points (200) ## - shapes(3): ##   - blobs_circles (5,circle) ##   - blobs_multipolygons (2,polygon) ##   - blobs_polygons (5,polygon) ## - tables(1): ##   - table (3,10) [blobs_labels] ## coordinate systems(5): ## - global(8): blobs_image blobs_multiscale_image ... blobs_polygons ##   blobs_points ## - scale(1): blobs_labels ## - translation(1): blobs_labels ## - affine(1): blobs_labels ## - sequence(1): blobs_labels

title: spatialdataR
text: SpatialData objects behave like nested list: first level corresponds layers, second level corresponds elements within layers. convenience, frequently needed accessor functions provided well. demonstrate various equivalent ways accessing layer element: Similarly, following equivalent ways retrieving element names:
code: # preferred way # (using accessor) image(sd, 1) image(sd, "blobs_image")   # alternative ways  # (using list-style) images(sd)[[1]] images(sd)[["blobs_image"]]  sd$images[[1]] sd$images$blobs_image sd$images[["blobs_image"]] # preferred way  # (using accessor) imageNames(sd)  # alternative ways  # (using list-style) names(sd[[1]]) names(sd$images) names(sd[["images"]])

title: spatialdataR
text: Object-wide subsetting SpatialData supported via [, can used drop layers, elements within layers. Note following operations -place, return SpatialData object fewer layers/elements:
code: # keep image-layer only sd[1] sd["images"]  # keep 1st image & label element sd[c(1, 2), list(1, 1)] sd[c("images", "labels"), list(1, 1)]

title: spatialdataR
text: Every spatial element (tables excluded) composed two key slots: data: list ZarrArrays images/labels, duckspatial_df shapes/points. meta: SpatialDataAttrs object containing OME-NGFF metadata retrieved zarr attributes present original Zarr store. demonstrate access slots given element image elements represent special case, stored list ZarrArrays (one per multi-scale resolution). , data() provides additional argument k specifies resolution retrieve: k=1 retrieves highest resolution (default). k=Inf retrieves lowest resolution available. k=NULL retrieves full list available resolutions.
code: # get multi-scale image i <- image(sd, 2)  a <- data(i, 1)   # highest b <- data(i, Inf) # lowest  # available resolutions l <- data(i, NULL)  # dimensions of each d <- vapply(l, dim, integer(3)) rownames(d) <- c("c", "y", "x") colnames(d) <- seq_along(l) show(d) ##    1  2  3 ## c  3  3  3 ## y 64 32 16 ## x 64 32 16

title: spatialdataR
text: key feature SpatialData framework handling different coordinate systems. element can exist multiple coordinate spaces simultaneously, defined transformations -disk Zarr attributes. relationships different elements respective coordinate spaces can complex. SpatialData provides CTgraph() CTplot() functions construct visualize directed graph relationships: source nodes (prefixed _) represent individual elements. target nodes represent coordinate systems (e.g., global). edges represent transformations required align element.  transform() function resolves necessary steps project element target coordinate system traversing graph, applying respective transformation(s). hood, involves: Retrieving relevant transformation data element’s SpatialDataAttr (e.g., scale factors x- y-coordinates); , Applying appropriate transformation function(s) correct order (e.g., scale() translation()).
code: g <- CTgraph(sd) CTplot(g) # get element  a <- label(sd)  # project into 'global' b <- spatialdataR::transform(a, "scale")  # compare XY extents do.call(rbind, c(a=extent(a), b=extent(b))) ##     [,1] [,2] ## a.x    0   64 ## a.y    0   64 ## b.x    0  128 ## b.y    0  192

title: spatialdataR
text: crop() may used subset elements – across layers – according spatial bounding box polygon. region may supplied different ways, including SpatialDataShape. addition, following okay: bounding box cropping, sf::st_bbox() object, list xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax values (order irrelevant). polygon cropping, sf::st_polygon() sf::st_sfc() object, two-column matrix XY coordinates (least 3 rows = triangle).
code: # bounding box xy <- list(xmin=-Inf, xmax=Inf, ymin=20, ymax=40) sp <- crop(sd, xy)  plot(     point(sd)$geometry, col="blue",      main="crop() with\nbounding box")  points(point(sp)$geometry, col="red")   # polygon xy <- rbind(c(0, 0), c(64, 0), c(32, 64)) sq <- crop(sd, xy)  plot(     point(sd)$geometry, col="blue",      main="crop() with\npolygon")  points(point(sq)$geometry, col="red")

title: spatialdataR
text: mask() aggregates data elements across layers, support masking points images labels, points shapes, shapes shapes: point shape masking counts number points fall within shape (e.g., counting transcripts cell membrane nucleus segmentation boundaries order obtain gene ×\times cell-level data). image label masking aggregates channel-wise pixel values image according regions defined label (e.g., obtaining mean fluorescence intensities per cell). shape shape masking aggregates data table one shape another shape (e.g., summarizing cell-level data regions interest). couple considerations also worth mentioning: identifier resulting table may specified via name, default i_by_j masking element element j. Instances map instances j (e.g., unassigned transcripts) assigned special “0” column resulting table.
code: # average channel-wise pixel values by labels sp <- mask(sd, i="blobs_image", j="blobs_labels") se <- table(sp, "blobs_image_by_blobs_labels") assay(se) ## 3 x 10 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix" ##                                                                             ## 0 0.1325593 0.11240838 0.05495268 0.13883753 0.1759411 0.05216844 0.1574298 ## 1 0.1773876 0.08582868 0.07500563 0.14309153 0.1848081 0.18733438 0.1321635 ## 2 0.1171959 0.13570302 0.18691482 0.06592828 0.1295153 0.03637876 0.1985580 ##                                       ## 0 0.0225221691 0.134730899 0.10671244 ## 1 0.2252634943 0.071093576 0.07564495 ## 2 0.0002233054 0.006969089 0.09928612 # count different point species in polygons sp <- mask(sd, i="blobs_points", j="blobs_polygons") se <- table(sp, "blobs_points_by_blobs_polygons") assay(se) ## 2 x 6 sparse Matrix of class "dgCMatrix" ##          0 1 2 3 4 5 ## gene_a  87 . . 1 2 . ## gene_b 105 2 . 1 . 2 # average shape-level data by other shapes sp <- mask(sd, i="blobs_polygons", j="blobs_circles")

title: spatialdataR
text: combine() can used merge two SpatialData objects one (many, via .call(list(...), combine)). , elements names made unique across objects via make.names(), appending suffix element names subsequent objects. Alternatively, names customize combining.
code: sp <- combine(list(foo=sd, bar=sd)) cbind(     original=lengths(colnames(sd)),     combined=lengths(colnames(sp))) ##        original combined ## images        2        4 ## labels        2        4 ## points        1        2 ## shapes        3        6 ## tables        1        2 imageNames(sp) ## [1] "foo.blobs_image"            "foo.blobs_multiscale_image" ## [3] "bar.blobs_image"            "bar.blobs_multiscale_image"

title: spatialdataR
text: centroids() may used extract spatial coordinates every instance given element. applies layers except images tables. Notably, labels shapes, centroids region returned (center mass). extent() obtain range element’s spatial coordinates target coordinate space. can done one element, object-wide order obtain largest extent across elements object.
code: head(centroids(point(sd))) ##    x  y  genes ## 1  6 48 gene_b ## 2 41 28 gene_b ## 3 27 54 gene_b ## 4  6 44 gene_a ## 5 13  6 gene_b ## 6 33 61 gene_b # object-wide xy <- extent(sd) unlist(xy) ## x1 x2 y1 y2  ##  0 64  0 64 # one element xy <- extent(point(sd)) unlist(xy) ## x1 x2 y1 y2  ##  1 62  1 62 # with prior alignment to target coordinate space xy <- extent(label(sd)) yx <- extent(label(sd), "scale") rbind(native=unlist(xy), scaled=unlist(yx)) ##        x1  x2 y1  y2 ## native  0  64  0  64 ## scaled  0 128  0 192

title: Representation of Python's spatialdata in R
text: --memory handling images labels using ZarrArray (via Rarr package). Points shapes managed using duckdb-backed tables. Functional annotations (e.g., gene expression) represented SingleCellExperiment objects, integrated via anndataR. system mapping data across multiple coordinate spaces, including support transformation graphs.

title: spatial cropping — crop
text: crop subsets SpatialData elements according rectangular bounding box arbitrary polygonal shapes. SpatialData objects, crop propagates operation across layers share coordinate space j. SpatialDataFrames (points shapes), cropping relies sf::st_intersects (.e., instances intersect query region way kept). circle shapes, radii currently ignored (.e., circle kept centroid intersects query region). SpatialDataArrays (images labels), bounding box cropping supported. requested spatial bounding box projected pixel coordinates, underlying array sliced accordingly. wh metadata updated reflect new spatial extent.

title: spatial cropping — crop
code: zs <- file.path("extdata", "blobs.zarr") zs <- system.file(zs, package="spatialdataR") sd <- readSpatialData(zs, tables=FALSE)  # bounding box crop of a SpatialData object y <- list(xmin=10, xmax=50, ymin=10, ymax=50) crop(sd, y, j="global") #> class: SpatialData #> - images(2): #>   - blobs_image (3,40,40) #>   - blobs_multiscale_image (3,40,40) #> - labels(2): #>   - blobs_labels (40,40) #>   - blobs_multiscale_labels (40,40) #> - points(1): #>   - blobs_points (92) #> - shapes(3): #>   - blobs_circles (5,circle) #>   - blobs_multipolygons (2,polygon) #>   - blobs_polygons (5,polygon) #> - tables(0): #> coordinate systems(5): #> - global(8): blobs_image blobs_multiscale_image ... blobs_polygons #>   blobs_points #> - scale(1): blobs_labels #> - translation(1): blobs_labels #> - affine(1): blobs_labels #> - sequence(1): blobs_labels  # cropping individual elements a <- sf::st_bbox(c(xmin=10, xmax=50, ymin=10, ymax=50)) b <- matrix(c(10,10, 25,50, 40,10, 10,10), ncol=2, byrow=TRUE) p <- crop(point(sd), a) q <- crop(point(sd), b)  plot(p$geometry, col="blue") plot(q$geometry, col="red", add=TRUE) plot(sf::st_as_sfc(a), add=TRUE) lines(b, type="l")
